summary: chain Pinned+WaitNoPop is structurally undersaturating cb_wait_front: chain only waits for 1 tile but reads tile at runtime idx=w. Original raw helpers (exp_tile_to_cb, mul_tiles_and_negative_to_cb) did cumulative cb_wait_front(cb, w+1). Pre-migration moreh_bin_chain_rt also had this pattern but tests reportedly passed — likely race that becomes visible with new T1.07/T1.08 chain stack. Fix: add explicit cb_wait_front(cb_X, w+1) before each Pinned-runtime-idx chain call in w.cpp and w_large.cpp.

fix_attempt: add cb_wait_front(cb_y, w+1) before T1.07 and T1.08 chains in w.cpp
outcome: NEUTRAL — same fail count as diagnostic baseline 24/69
note: Pre-existing pattern: moreh_bin_chain_rt callsites (already present before 7dd7cf3824a) also use Pinned+WaitNoPop with runtime idx. Either Tensix CB protocol has hidden cumulative-visibility, or the bug is elsewhere. Fix reverted to keep kernel at HEAD state.
